column1: "Founded" dates are approximate and refer to the formalization or major emergence of the school.
Many schools are now historical or have been absorbed into others.
This table focuses on East Asian Mahāyāna, Chan/Zen, Pure Land, and related traditions as requested. For Tibetan, Theravāda, and other South Asian schools, see separate tables.
Modern movements (e.g., Humanistic Buddhism, Plum Village) are included for completeness.
This table provides a structured overview of the rich diversity and history of East Asian Buddhist schools and sects, including their doctrinal focus and development179.
